internal_issues.description: Mischaracterizes dome's RAR argument. Should address: dome has no RAR derivation, no galaxy-scale physics, no ontology for galaxies within 20,015 km disc.

suggested_patch.find: The claim cites Mistele (2024) but does not provide a lensing mechanism within dome geometry. Relativistic lensing around massive objects (galaxy clusters, stars) relies on spacetime curvature. A flat-dome model has no curved spacetime, no description of how electromagnetic radiation bends, and no gravitational potential formalism that produces lensing. Citing a paper is not the same as providing a self-consistent lensing model.
suggested_patch.replace: The dome cites Mistele et al. (2024, ApJL 969, L3) showing the Radial Acceleration Relation extends from kinematic to weak-lensing scales. This is a genuine empirical challenge to ΛCDM. However, the dome model provides no mathematical derivation of the RAR from aetheric physics — no equation for aetheric acceleration as a function of baryonic mass, no prediction of the RAR's slope or scatter. The dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km; galaxies observed at tens of kiloparsecs have no location in this geometry. Without an ontology for extragalactic objects, claims about their dynamical properties are incoherent. The dome hijacks a legitimate open question in cosmology without contributing any predictive framework.

internal_issues.description: FABRICATED DOI: 10.1038/ngeo2516 resolves to Doetterl et al. about soil carbon, not Stephens et al. on energy balance.
+0.34 W/m² is a decadal TREND, not static hemispheric difference. Stephens' key finding is hemispheric SYMMETRY.

suggested_patch.find: The hemispheric energy asymmetry is a well-studied consequence of land-ocean distribution: the northern hemisphere has 39% land vs 19% in the south. Land has lower heat capacity and albedo differences. Stephens et al. (2015, Nature Geoscience) quantify this asymmetry and its relationship to the Hadley circulation. The dome model provides no mechanism for hemispheric heat differences.
suggested_patch.replace: The hemispheric energy budget is remarkably symmetric — a key finding of Stephens et al. (2012, Nature Geoscience). The +0.34 W/m² figure cited by the dome is a decadal trend in Earth's energy imbalance (Raghuraman et al., 2025, PNAS), driven by aerosol changes, Arctic ice loss, and cloud shifts — not a static hemispheric difference. The northern hemisphere has ~39% land vs ~19% in the south, producing differential thermal inertia, albedo, and evaporation rates. While the dome's disc geometry is inherently asymmetric (NP at center), no radiation budget calculation on the dome site translates this geometry into a specific W/m² prediction.

internal_issues.description: Says satellites orbit 'above the alleged dome' — wrong. Swarm/GRACE at 250-500 km are BELOW firmament at ~9,086 km.

suggested_patch.find: as measured by Swarm and GRACE satellites orbiting at 250–500 km altitude (above the alleged dome).
suggested_patch.replace: as measured by Swarm and GRACE satellites orbiting at 250–500 km altitude — well below the dome's alleged firmament of ~9,086 km, meaning they measure gravitational acceleration within the dome's own predicted field.

internal_issues.description: Post-glacial rebound explanation incomplete. Primary explanation is mantle plume (Einarsson et al. 2020 PNAS), not just rebound.

suggested_patch.find: The Antarctic gravity low is explained by post-glacial rebound (ice sheet removal) — measured by GRACE satellites orbiting at 250–500 km altitude above the alleged dome.
suggested_patch.replace: The Antarctic gravity low is primarily explained by an isostatically uncompensated mantle plume beneath the East Antarctic Plateau, with contributions from post-glacial isostatic adjustment. Standard geophysics explains the feature without invoking undefined 'sump nodes.'

internal_issues.description: El Gordo detail_evidence vague. No specific velocity dispersion, no ΛCDM simulation comparison, no engagement with dome's actual '2,500 km/s' claim.
Verdict 'Misleading' may be wrong. Need research on El Gordo status in ΛCDM before patching.
WIN-058 undefined on dome site. Our argument is against our own interpretation, not dome's actual claim.
